question.text: Under SEBI LODR, what constitutes a special resolution requirement related to material subsidiaries?

answer.text: Appointment of independent directors on the subsidiary board
Disposal of more than 20% of the assets of a material subsidiary
Any related party transaction exceeding Rs.1 crore with the subsidiary
Change of auditors of the material subsidiary

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI LODR requires a special resolution from the shareholders of the listed entity for disposal of more than 20% of the assets of a material subsidiary. This protects minority shareholders from value erosion through subsidiary asset sales. **The Trap:** Appointment of independent directors on subsidiary boards is a board-level requirement, not a special resolution matter. Related party transactions have separate thresholds and approval mechanisms. **Cross-Exam Context:** This corporate governance provision under LODR ensures that significant transactions involving material subsidiaries receive shareholder scrutiny, not just board approval. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** RTAs processing extraordinary general meeting notices should flag resolutions involving disposal of material subsidiary assets. The 20% threshold requires careful valuation and disclosure.
